summary_text: **Requirements Packet** Objective: make local `.pdf` files open with `Chrome Canary` by default on this Mac, while keeping `Stirling-PDF` installed and manually launchable after the non-destructive repair already performed. In scope: macOS `LaunchServices` PDF associations, Finder double-click behavior, `open <file>.pdf` behavior, and verification that `Stirling-PDF` still starts normally after the state reset. Out of scope: uninstalling either app, deleting user data, modifying app binaries, changing unrelated browser or mail defaults, CI/GitHub work, or any destructive cleanup. Constraints: changes must be reversible, backed up, and limited to user-level preference/state updates. Do not assume `open` behavior and Finder behavior are identical. Do not delete anything without explicit approval. Acceptance criteria: Finder double-click on any local `.pdf` opens `Chrome Canary`; `open file.pdf` also resolves to `Chrome Canary`; `Stirling-PDF` remains launchable manually; `Stirling-PDF` does not immediately crash after the repair; all changes can be rolled back from the retained backups. Failure modes: Finder still uses a stale per-file or per-app association; `LaunchServices` cache remains stale after updates; `Chrome Canary` lacks a valid registration for one of the PDF identifiers; `Stirling-PDF` instability is in the app bundle rather than local state; repair removes recoverable user state instead of only resetting it. Stop conditions: if Finder still opens `Stirling-PDF` after cache refresh and association repair; if `Stirling-PDF` requires destructive removal to stabilize; if a change would require deleting unrecoverable data; if `Chrome Canary` cannot be made the effective handler without side effects. Completion proof: a fresh local `.pdf` opened from Finder launches `Chrome Canary`; `open /path/to/file.pdf` also launches `Chrome Canary`; `Stirling-PDF` starts successfully on manual launch; retained backups exist and can restore prior state. Best execution plan: verify all relevant PDF handler entries, flush or refresh `LaunchServices` and Finder state, test Finder double-click on a known local PDF, then only if needed inspect per-file metadata or stale handler caches. Keep the `Stirling-PDF` repair path separate and non-destructive, using the retained backup set as rollback insurance.
